channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Agents CLI in Agent Platform: create to production in one CLI
item.description: Google Cloud has launched the Agents CLI, a tool that simplifies the process of developing and deploying AI agents by integrating various Google Cloud services. This innovation aims to enhance productivity for developers by reducing complexity and improving efficiency in moving from concept to production.

item.title: Linux may get a hall pass from one state age-check bill, but Congress plays hall monitor
item.description: Colorado's proposed amendments may exempt open source operating systems from new age-check legislation, addressing concerns from FOSS advocates. However, stricter federal regulations are being considered in Congress, which could impact the future of open source software.

item.title: Google brings Auto Browse and Skills to Chrome Enterprise - and a new 'Gemini Summary'
item.description: Google has enhanced Chrome Enterprise with new AI features, including task automation and improved security controls, aiming to create a more efficient workspace for businesses. The introduction of Gemini Summary further supports users by streamlining workflows.

item.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI coworker for the workplace
item.description: With the introduction of Gemini-powered 'auto browse' capabilities, Google is turning Chrome into an AI coworker for enterprise users, automating tasks such as research and data entry. This feature aims to enhance workplace efficiency.

item.title: Datacenter boom keeps dirty coal plants alive in the US
item.description: Research indicates that the growth of data centers in the US is prolonging the operation of older coal plants, hindering the transition to cleaner energy sources and exacerbating air pollution. This raises environmental concerns amid increasing digital infrastructure demands.

item.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent building tool for enterprises
item.description: Google's new Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform is tailored for IT and technical users, offering a unique approach to building AI agents for enterprises. This focus aims to enhance the capabilities of technical teams in deploying AI solutions.

item.title: New Firefox update patches a whopping 271 bugs, thanks to Claude Mythos
item.description: The latest Firefox update addresses 271 security vulnerabilities and introduces enhancements like split view and tab sharing, improving user experience and safety. Users are encouraged to update to benefit from these fixes.

item.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
item.description: Gmail is set to introduce AI Overviews, which will provide users with instant summaries from multiple emails, streamlining communication and information retrieval in the workplace. This feature aims to enhance productivity for users.

item.title: Workday, Rippling, and Slack flunk data access test, claims Fivetran
item.description: A report by Fivetran criticizes Workday, Rippling, and Slack for poor performance in data access and integration, impacting analytics and AI capabilities. This highlights the challenges enterprises face in effective data management.

item.title: After testing this Garmin rival, I won't use a smartwatch without a built-in flashlight again
item.description: The Polar Street X smartwatch, featuring a built-in LED flashlight, offers a competitive alternative to Garmin at an affordable price, enhancing functionality for users. This innovation could influence future smartwatch designs.

item.title: Thunderbird in hand worth 2 Outlooks as fresh FOSS fave and Firefox arrive
item.description: Thunderbird remains a top choice for users needing reliable email support, especially in challenging situations like remote locations. Its continued relevance highlights the importance of open-source solutions in tech.

item.title: AI Tools Are Helping Mediocre North Korean Hackers Steal Millions
item.description: North Korean hackers have leveraged AI to enhance their cyberattacks, reportedly stealing up to $12 million in a short period. This highlights the growing sophistication of cybercrime using advanced technologies.

item.title: Company-wise memory in Amazon Bedrock with Amazon Neptune and Mem0
item.description: Amazon Bedrock now features company-specific memory capabilities, allowing AI agents to provide tailored interactions based on persistent context. This innovation aims to enhance user experience in customer service applications.

item.title: Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super
item.description: The Gemma 4 VLA Demo on Jetson Orin Nano Super showcases advanced AI capabilities in a compact form factor. This highlights the potential for powerful AI applications in smaller devices.

item.title: You can now run WSL on Windows 95, in case you're crazy, too
item.description: A developer has successfully run the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) on Windows 95, showcasing impressive technical skills. This hack illustrates the creativity and determination within the tech community.

item.title: 5 gifts your mom will actually like for Mother's Day
item.description: A guide to Mother's Day gifts features gadgets and cozy items that are well-received by moms. This reflects the ongoing trend of tech gifts being popular among consumers.

item.title: This Moto G Stylus phone deal includes a free smartwatch and more - here's how it works
item.description: Motorola is offering a deal on the Moto G Stylus that includes a free smartwatch and accessories. This promotion aims to attract customers by adding value to their purchase.

item.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
item.description: OpenAI has partnered with Infosys to integrate AI tools into business processes, focusing on software development and automation. This collaboration aims to modernize and streamline operations for clients.

item.title: I set up this Linux 'Watchdog' and now my system auto-reboots when it locks up
item.description: A Linux 'Watchdog' tool has been set up to automatically reboot unresponsive systems, enhancing reliability. This practical solution addresses common issues faced by Linux users.

item.title: NASA reckons the Artemis II heat shield performed like a champ
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission confirmed that the Orion heat shield performed effectively during re-entry, providing confidence for future missions. This success is crucial for the agency's ongoing exploration goals.

item.title: Lenovo vs. Dell: My buying advice after testing dozens of laptops from both
item.description: This article compares Dell and Lenovo laptops, highlighting their strengths and weaknesses to help consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: Watch Sony’s elite ping-pong robot beat top-ranked players
item.description: Sony's new ping-pong robot, developed by its AI division, can compete with and occasionally defeat top-ranked human players, showcasing advancements in robotics.

item.title: An AI app prepares me for my day now - and I've never been more organized
item.description: The Huxe app organizes personal schedules and news into a daily briefing, helping users stay organized and informed.

item.title: From Rainforests to Recycling Plants: 5 Ways NVIDIA AI Is Protecting the Planet
item.description: NVIDIA AI is being used in various applications aimed at environmental protection, including climate monitoring and recycling efforts.

item.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
item.description: Startup 10x Science has secured $4.8 million in funding to help researchers identify significant drug candidates from the increasing number generated by AI.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos rollout has missed America’s cybersecurity agency
item.description: Anthropic's new cybersecurity model, Mythos, is being adopted by several federal agencies, but the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency is notably absent.

item.title: Google Meet will take AI notes for in-person meetings too
item.description: Google Meet's AI notetaker now extends to in-person meetings, generating summaries and transcripts for various platforms, enhancing meeting productivity.

item.title: Google bets $32B on AI agent cyber force as security arms race escalates
item.description: Google is investing $32 billion in AI agents to enhance cybersecurity, preparing for a future where machines combat cyber threats at unprecedented speeds.

item.title: Google claims to have all the answers for enterprise AI agent sprawl
item.description: Google is revamping its enterprise AI strategy to address the growing complexity of AI agents in business, launching the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.

item.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
item.description: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated to create a comprehensive AI platform, enabling the deployment of advanced AI technologies in various sectors.

item.title: Speeding up agentic workflows with WebSockets in the Responses API
item.description: The article discusses improvements in the Codex agent loop by utilizing WebSockets and connection-scoped caching, which enhance API performance and reduce latency. This optimization is significant for developers relying on efficient model interactions.

item.title: Introducing workspace agents in ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT has introduced workspace agents powered by Codex, designed to automate complex workflows and facilitate secure collaboration across tools in the cloud. This development aims to improve team productivity and streamline operations.

item.title: Workspace agents
item.description: This article provides a guide on building and scaling workspace agents in ChatGPT to automate repetitive tasks and enhance team operations. It emphasizes the integration of various tools for better workflow management.

item.title: Introducing OpenAI Privacy Filter
item.description: OpenAI has launched a Privacy Filter model that accurately detects and redacts personally identifiable information (PII) from text. This tool is significant for enhancing data privacy in AI applications.

item.title: Apple Machine Learning Research at ICLR 2026
item.description: Apple is actively contributing to AI and machine learning research, participating in the ICLR 2026 conference to share advancements and support the research community. This involvement highlights Apple's commitment to AI innovation.

item.title: Show HN: Partial-zod – streaming JSON parser for LLMs (zero deps, Zod-native)
item.description: The article presents Partial-zod, a streaming JSON parser designed for LLM outputs with zero runtime dependencies and native adapters for various AI platforms. This tool simplifies data handling for developers.

item.title: COVID scientist accused of using ChatGPT in alleged poisoning plot against rival
item.description: A scientist from UW-Madison is accused of using ChatGPT to plot the poisoning of a colleague, allegedly due to professional grievances. This case raises ethical concerns about AI's role in criminal activities.

item.title: This Frankenstein AI Merges Claude Opus, GLM and Qwen—And Outperforms Top Models
item.description: Kyle Hessling has created a 'frankenmerge' AI model by combining two finetunes, achieving performance that surpasses leading models. This innovation showcases the potential of model integration in AI development.

item.title: social-research-probe 0.0.7
item.description: The social-research-probe 0.0.7 is a command-line interface for evidence-based social media research, integrating Claude Code skills. This tool aims to enhance research methodologies in social sciences.

item.title: watchingai 0.8.1
item.description: The watchingai 0.8.1 is a desktop widget that displays the working status of Claude Code, providing users with real-time updates. This tool enhances user experience by keeping track of AI model activity.

item.title: toc-cluster added to PyPI
item.description: The toc-cluster has been added to PyPI, enhancing clustering capabilities with a focus on trust and confidence in data analysis. This addition is significant for developers seeking reliable clustering tools.

item.title: boltzina added to PyPI
item.description: Boltzina, a new tool added to PyPI, offers efficient virtual screening through docking-guided binding prediction. This advancement is crucial for drug discovery and development.

item.title: Adobe wants to help your brand get recognized even when search is getting replaced by AI
item.description: Adobe launched CX Enterprise at the Adobe Summit 2026, aiming to enhance brand recognition in an AI-driven search landscape. This system manages the entire customer lifecycle, marking a significant shift in customer experience management.

item.title: AIRIX – See if ChatGPT recommends your business with a free 5-second scan
item.description: AIRIX offers a free 5-second scan to see if ChatGPT recommends a business, providing a quick assessment tool for brand visibility. This service highlights the growing influence of AI in business recommendations.

item.title: Insilico Medicine Announces Industry's First Longevity Board to Accelerate AI-Driven Aging Research for Drug Discovery
item.description: Insilico Medicine has formed the industry's first Longevity Board to accelerate AI-driven aging research for drug discovery. This initiative aims to enhance the development of therapies targeting age-related diseases.

item.title: qortara-governance-langchain 0.1.0
item.description: The Qortara Governance LangChain sidecar SDK has been released, featuring deep tool-dispatch interception and policy enforcement capabilities. This tool is designed to enhance governance in AI applications.

item.title: qortara-governance-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Qortara Governance has been added to PyPI, providing a sidecar SDK for LangChain that includes features like portable trust attestations. This addition supports developers in building compliant AI systems.

item.title: tracetorch 0.18.2
item.description: The release of Tracetorch 0.18.2 offers a powerful library for spiking neural networks and recurrent neural networks in PyTorch. This tool is significant for researchers working on advanced neural network architectures.

item.title: Trellis AI (YC W24) Is hiring engineers to build self-improving agents
item.description: Trellis AI is hiring engineers to develop self-improving agents that help patients access life-saving medicines. This initiative aims to automate healthcare processes, improving patient outcomes.

item.title: Titan’s strange plains may be explained by unusual weather
item.description: Research suggests that Titan's unusually flat and smooth surface may be due to a layer of fluffy organic material deposited from its atmosphere. This finding could reshape our understanding of Titan's geological processes.
